---
title: "About"
---

## Mark (Yu) Zhang, PhD, RPF

I am a Registered Professional Forester (RPF) and forest analyst with over 15 years
of combined experience in academic ecological research and applied forest management
in Ontario. My career spans from peer-reviewed global-scale ecological research to
frontline forest management planning across three Ontario MNR districts.

Since joining the Ontario public service in 2017, I have been a member of FMP
planning teams for seven Forest Management Units across Northwest, Northeast, and
Southern Ontario — covering base model setup, yield curve fitting, long-term
management direction, and stakeholder consultation including Indigenous community
engagement. My current focus is on wood supply modeling, spatial data tool
development, and analytical automation to support upcoming FMP planning cycles
in the northeast GLSL forest region.

The tools on this site were built out of practical necessity — to fill gaps in
existing workflows for data validation, yield curve modeling, LiDAR analysis, and
forest unit classification.

## Education

| Degree | Institution | Year |
|---|---|---|
| PhD, Forest Sciences | Lakehead University, Thunder Bay, ON | 2014 |
| MSc, Forestry | Lakehead University, Thunder Bay, ON | 2010 |
| HBSc, Agriculture (Horticulture) | University of Guelph, ON | 2008 |

## Professional Registration

- **Registered Professional Forester (RPF)** — Ontario Professional Foresters Association (OPFA), Member 

## Research Background (2008–2017)

Prior to joining the Ontario public service, I was an active researcher in boreal
forest ecosystem dynamics, with a focus on biodiversity–productivity relationships
across global forest biomes. My postdoctoral work was conducted at Lakehead
University and the South China Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences.

My **5 first-author papers** have accumulated **1,259 citations** (as of April 2026,
per Semantic Scholar), averaging ~252 citations per paper. The 2012 *Journal of
Ecology* meta-analysis alone has been cited by primary research papers published
in *Science* (2016) and *Nature* (2017).

## Areas of Technical Expertise

- Forest inventory analysis (FRI/PCI/BMI/OPI — Ontario MNRF standards)
- Explicit spatial forest estate modeling (e.g., Woodstock and FSOS)
- LiDAR data processing and 3D visualization
- Yield curve modeling (Advanced user of SLYM & BMI-based fitting)
- Interactive web application development (R/Shiny, JavaScript)
- Spatial data workflows and GIS automation (ArcGIS Pro, Python, R)
- Data visualization and reporting (Power BI, Quarto, Leaflet)

## Tools & Technologies

| Category | Technologies |
|---|---|
| Languages | R, JavaScript (ES2020+), PowerShell, Python |
| Frameworks | Shiny, Quarto, Leaflet, terra, lidR |
| Forest Modeling | SFMM/FSOS, Patchworks, Woodstock, SLYM, MIST |
| Spatial | ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, Ontario GeoHub APIs, WebGL |
| Visualization | Power BI (DAX, Power Query), ggplot2, Leaflet |
| Workflow | Git, GitHub, CSV/Shapefile/LiDAR pipelines |
